hero_sub_technical: // a utility that reads your playlists, detects the genres, and splits them into new, clean ones — in seconds, not evenings.
hero_sub_note: Free. Open source. No ads. No model training. Works on every playlist you own or follow.
hero_cta_note: ◇ no card · no signup · oauth only
how_step1_title: We read every track in your playlist.
how_step1_body: Spotify oauth token, scoped to read + modify. Nothing is stored server-side. Your library stays yours.
how_step2_title: We pull the genres from each song.
how_step2_body: Artist metadata, audio features, collaborative tags. We run them through a dedup pass and land on ~40 canonical buckets.
how_step3_title: You pick. We build the new playlists.
how_step3_body: Select any combination of genres. Sortify spins up fresh playlists on your Spotify, titled and ordered. Keep, rename, delete.
faq_a1: Yes. Sortify is free and will stay free. It costs us about €4/month to run. No paywalls, no premium tier, no dark patterns.
faq_a2: No. We use Spotify oauth to read your playlists on-the-fly, compute the genres in memory, and write the new playlists back. Nothing is persisted server-side beyond session duration. Full source is on github if you want to verify.
faq_a3: Good enough. We look up each track on Last.fm and use the community-voted genre tags to classify it. Results are cached so repeat runs are instant. Edge cases exist — a pop-punk track might land in "rock" or "punk" depending on how the community tagged it. You can re-run at any time.
faq_q4: Will it modify my original playlist?
faq_a4: Never. Sortify only reads the source playlist and creates new ones. Your originals are untouched. You can delete the generated ones from Spotify anytime if you don't like them.
faq_q5: Why is it only for Spotify?
faq_a5: Because that's what we use. If you'd like to see it on Apple Music, Deezer, or Tidal, open an issue or send a PR. The core sort logic is platform-agnostic.
faq_a6: Yes. The repo includes a docker-compose and a small python worker. You'll need to register your own Spotify developer app. Takes ~10 minutes if you've done oauth before.
cta_title: Free. Open source. Yours to fork.
cta_body: Sortify is MIT-licensed and runs on a single python worker. Read the source, host your own, or ship a PR.
footer_tagline: A small utility for people with too much music and not enough patience.
dash_sub: // choose a playlist to sort. sortify will read its tracks, detect genres, and split it into new playlists on your spotify.
dash_missing_sub: Spotify restricts API access to Blends, Daylist, Wrapped and other playlists they own. To sort one of those, copy its tracks into a new playlist first. You can also paste any user-created playlist link below.
dash_lookup_error_invalid: Invalid Spotify playlist URL or ID.
dash_lookup_error_not_found: Playlist not found — check the link and try again.
create_sub: // choose the genres you want in your new playlist by clicking them. sortify will write a fresh playlist to your spotify with just those tracks — your source stays untouched.
create_no_genres: No genres found. Try sorting the playlist again.
sort_sub_technical: // fetching the genres of your music, hold tight.
sort_sub_note: up to 500ms per track · no data leaves this worker
sort_error: Could not connect to the sorting service.
done_sub_technical: // your new playlist has been written to your spotify.
done_next_sub: // do it again with a different source, or head back to the dashboard to review all your playlists.
done_tip: tip · press D for dashboard · R to run again
notfound_msg: // there is nothing there. the page you are looking for does not exist or has been moved.
